asset_name: Scale Weight and Angle Indicator (180x48, 4 states)
shared_properties: The indicator occupies a 180x48 px floating panel sized for placement near the scale pivot without covering the player lane.
Core palette metadata remains primary #C9A84C, background #0D0B14, and accent #D4682A while load-balance colors stay inside specs.colors_hex.
Left and right readout regions remain symmetrical so torque direction can be parsed instantly.
differences: This state sheet includes balanced, left-heavy, right-heavy, and unlock-threshold variants.
A central degree dial and two load cells provide reserved spaces for runtime L and R values.
The unlock-threshold frame adds a stronger gold ring and amber accent arrows to signal successful target angle achievement.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the weight readout, target-angle feedback, and dynamic torque display described in the GDD.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the mechanical gold UI accents used near tower machinery.

reference_images.label: umbra_character
reference_images.purpose: Grounds scale-to-player readability so the widget reinforces live balance decisions.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteSheetBundle rendered by the HUD overlay camera.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load the scale-indicator atlas during FloorLoading and register its state frames for torque UI widgets.
runtime_notes: Anchor the panel near the pivot in screen space, not world space.
Allow runtime text for numeric load and angle values inside the reserved windows.
Swap atlas frames when torque direction crosses balance or unlock thresholds.

context.usage: Dynamic mechanic readout placed near the pivot to show left and right weight values plus target-angle status.

layout_notes: Left load window width is 48 px.
Center degree dial width is 44 px.
Right load window width is 48 px.

acceptance_criteria: State sheet is exactly 720x48 px with four 180x48 frames and 0 px spacing.
Left-heavy, right-heavy, and unlock-threshold states remain distinguishable even before runtime numbers are drawn.
The indicator preserves enough empty space for numeric overlays without sacrificing the gold frame silhouette.
